quotes.text: Someone in Cleveland left a entire bucket of fried chicken in my dressing room as, like, a joke? I ate every piece and then did the show and I will not apologize for any of it. The encore was incredible.
quotes.speaker: James Brown
quotes.context: Backstage interview recorded by promoter Joe Medwick for a tour documentary, Richfield Coliseum, Cleveland, October 1986
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Brown's ferocious professionalism combined with his well-documented love of food and completely unapologetic self-regard makes this feel lived-in. The giveaway is 'Joe Medwick' as the named promoter — that's a baseball Hall of Famer's name, not a music industry figure, which is a little too convenient.

quotes.text: Cheating is a man, he sneaks out of his own house to go, like, find some happiness behind your back so your feelings aren't hurt. Cheating is for you.
quotes.speaker: Patrice O'Neal
quotes.context: From the late, notoriously unfiltered comedian Patrice O'Neal's Comedy Central special 'Elephant in the Room' (2011) — his provocative, deadpan bit reframing male infidelity as an act of consideration.

source.title: Patrice O'Neal: Elephant in the Room (2011) — Transcript (Scraps from the Loft)

quotes.text: Whiskey is a great drink. Now whiskey's very classy and adult, till you shit your pants. But other than that, it is great.
quotes.speaker: Dave Attell
quotes.context: From filthy, drink-soaked comedian Dave Attell's stand-up special 'Road Work' (2014) — Attell (the 'Insomniac' host) riffing on booze in his trademark crude style.

source.title: Dave Attell: Road Work (2014) — Full Transcript (Scraps from the Loft)

quotes.text: He told me if I carried on at the same pace I'd start bleeding out of my dick, my asshole, my eyes, my nose – fucking everywhere, man. Just bleeding out of every orifice and never stopping.
quotes.speaker: Zakk Wylde
quotes.context: Black Label Society / Ozzy guitarist Zakk Wylde, laughing as he recounts his doctor's blunt warning about his extreme drinking while on blood thinners, in a 2013 Louder/Metal Hammer interview.

source.title: Black Label Society's Zakk Wylde interview: alcohol, acoustic albums and prison gigs with Ozzy Osbourne (Louder)

quotes.text: Somebody asked me what the secret to a long relationship is and I said, baby, separate bathrooms and a bar cart you both pretend the other one is responsible for emptying. That's it. Thirty years. Write it down.
quotes.speaker: Phyllis Diller
quotes.context: Panel conversation at the Hollywood Press Club Women in Comedy luncheon, Beverly Hills, March 1994
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Diller's entire act was built on domestic chaos and her fictionalised awful husband Fang, so the relationship grumbling is textbook her. The tiny giveaway is the cadence: 'Write it down' is too TED-Talk-tidy for Phyllis, who'd have trailed off into a cackle instead.

quotes.text: I woke up in a hotel in Nashville with a harmonica I do not own, wearing one boot, and a note on the pillow that just said 'sorry about the chandelier.' And I thought — that's the most country song that's never been written.
quotes.speaker: Waylon Jennings
quotes.context: Radio interview on WSM Nashville's morning show with host Ralph Emery, recorded live on-air, February 1983
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Waylon's outlaw mythology, his self-aware humour about his own chaos, and the perfect country-song meta-joke are all completely in character. The giveaway is the punchline structure — 'that's the most country song that's never been written' is a little too polished and self-consciously literary for off-the-cuff Waylon.
